intro: Orobus komarovii, also known as Komarov's vetch, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Orobus komarovii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Orobus komarovii is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
